seoTitle: How to View a Shared Calendar in Outlook
metaDescription: Add a coworker's calendar to your Outlook view by searching the directory, requesting access, and using overlay mode to compare schedules at a glance.
intro: Coordinating meetings shouldn't require an endless back-and-forth of availability emails. This guide covers finding a coworker's calendar in your directory, requesting access if needed, and using overlay mode to stack multiple schedules into one view.

steps.heading: Open your calendar view
steps.body: Launch Outlook and navigate to the primary calendar board layout.

steps.heading: Add a calendar from the directory
steps.body: Click Add Calendar in the ribbon and select "From Directory."

steps.heading: Search for the coworker
steps.body: Type their name or email into the search field to locate their profile.

steps.heading: Select and file it
steps.body: Choose the target profile and pick a group or folder location to keep it organized.

steps.heading: Request access if needed
steps.body: If details are hidden, use the Request Permission option to send an authorization note.

steps.heading: Overlay the calendars
steps.body: Activate Overlay View mode to combine the shared schedule with your own for easy comparison.

faq.question: What if I can't find a coworker's calendar in the directory?
faq.answer: Their calendar visibility may be restricted by permissions, in which case you may need to send a formal access request.

faq.question: What does Overlay View actually do?
faq.answer: It stacks multiple calendars on top of each other in a single grid so you can compare availability at a glance instead of switching tabs.

faq.question: Can I group several team members' calendars together?
faq.answer: Yes, you can bundle multiple shared calendars into a dedicated calendar group for easier ongoing access.

faq.question: Will changes to a coworker's calendar show up automatically?
faq.answer: Shared calendar data is designed to sync in near real time, though refreshing your view can help confirm the latest updates.
